title: langloisia setosissima subsp. punctata
intro: Langloisia setosissima subsp. punctata, also known as the San Diego encinitas bells, is a rare and endemic perennial plant native to the coastal sage scrub of San Diego County in California. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties. The plant is believed to provide health benefits such as reducing inflammation, soothing digestive issues, and supporting immune function. Herbal preparations of Langloisia setosissima subsp. punctata are typically made by infusing the plant's leaves and flowers in hot water or oil, and can be used as a tea, tincture, or topical salve.
